name: Mccormick's Minestrone
recipeIngredient: 1 cup dry beans (Great Northern, kidney, pinto, lima, or mixture)
28 ounces whole tomatoes, cut into small pieces
1/2 lb spinach, washed, stems removed, and torn into bite-size pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over beans to remove stones and empty shells. Place in a 4-quart bowl, add 2 quarts water, and soak overnight. Drain and rinse thoroughly.
2. If using the quick-soaking method on the package of beans, follow those instructions instead.
3. In a Dutch oven or large saucepan, combine 2 quarts fresh water and beans. Heat to boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 2 1/2 to 3 hours or until beans are tender.
4. Heat oil in a skillet. Add celery, green bell pepper, chopped onions, parsley, and minced garlic. Sauté but do not brown. Stir into the beans.
5. Add remaining ingredients except water and cheese to beans. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
6. Add 4 to 6 additional cups water, depending on desired consistency. Cook until heated through.
7. Ladle into soup bowls and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
This is a thick soup. If desired, thin with additional boiling water to reach the desired consistency.
Makes 12 servings, 1 cup each or 6 servings of 2 cups.
